Description: Improve an existing plan by refining tasks, adding details, or restructuring for clarity and efficiency.

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. If the file contains only the legacy shape `{ "plan_path": "..." }`, treat that as an empty `additional_repositories` list. The current repository is the canonical plan host and is implicitly in scope, so it must not appear inside `additional_repositories`. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository, that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename, and that every additional repository is either already on that same story branch or can safely create or reuse that branch without overwriting local changes. If any of those checks fail, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Do not independently search for or select a different plan. Read the plan's `### Additional Repositories` section and ensure every repository named by the tasks comes from either `Current Repository` or that section. Does the selected story contain tasks? If not, please create tasks for this story, ensuring that each task only implements one thing that can be tested. Server changes to messages should be tasked separately and prior to the front-end changes that rely on them unless changing the server will break the front end. Ensure that each task is detailed enough for a junior developer to work through without the input of a senior. Base the formatting, detail, and structure of the tasks on the `plan_format.md` file within this repository (search for it yourself). Every task MUST explicitly name the repository it belongs to, following the current plan format. If the story spans multiple repositories, a single task MUST only belong to one repository, so split provider-repository and consumer-repository work into separate tasks and make the sequencing between them explicit. If a library repository and one or more consuming repositories are all changing, task the library work separately from each consuming repository. If it is already tasked up, then do not just state this and stop; ensure the existing tasks still follow these repository rules and then give an overview of the tasks. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

If the file `codeinfo_markdown/repository_information.md` exists, then read it as it will contain useful information you should keep referencing while working on a plan in this repository. If the file does not exist, then don't worry about it. Tell me if you found the file or not.

Does the tasking up reflect all the work required to be implemented as part of this story's requirements and details or are there parts missing? If there are parts missing, please add them as new tasks or subtasks as required. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

If this plan requires new test harnesses to be created, ensure that each one is properly defined and detailed within it's own task and that there is at least one test that proves the harness can be executed and checked for errors. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Please perform a feasability proof pass over the tasks. For each task in turn, confirm whether the required capabilities already exist as previous tasks, in the relevant repositories, or whether they must be first created as explicit prerequisite work. you MUST distinguish between: 1. Already existing capabilities,  2. Missing prerequisite capabilities,  3. Assumptions that are currently invalid. Validate these prerequisites per repository, not just globally. If the story spans multiple repositories, reject any task that mixes work across more than one repository, split it into repository-specific tasks, and ensure cross-repository prerequisites are added early enough in the correct repository before downstream tasks depend on them. Update the plan so that any missing tasks are added early enough to be used by the tasks that require them. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you can based on what you found while researching. Commit after making any changes.

Use the code_info, Context7, DeepWiki, and MUI MCP servers, as well as internet searches, to gather repository evidence and current library documentation for any assumptions made about library usage, interfaces, components, and similar details. Note that if the library is available in code_info, then it is likely to be the correct version of the library, and information from there should supersede other MCP servers. You MUST use the tools and never make assumptions, even for things like React, TypeScript, Mongo, and Mongoose, remembering to provide the name of the repository when you ask the question to code_info. Then inspect the relevant source files directly and decide whether the assumptions in the plan are valid and what should change. If a controlled library repository is changing alongside one or more consuming repositories, inspect both the library and the consuming repositories, keep the work in separate repository-specific tasks, and add testing/proof steps that use `npm link` where appropriate so the changed library is exercised by the changed downstream repository before publish. Update the plan and tasks based on what you find. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Some of the tasks look quite big; could they be split up without losing any information? Please split up any large tasks into smaller, more manageable subtasks where possible, especially if they have a title with the word 'and' in it, but only break up tasks if it doesn't make the work more complex or untestable. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Are there any complex or risky items that we could consider simplifying to de-risk? Please simplify the instructions where possible to reduce complexity and risk, focusing on nice-to-have things that are risky and complex to implement, but don't simplify for the sake of it if there are no changes that would help. Also, we must ensure we still meet all the acceptance criteria of the story plan. Do NOT remove testing steps as part of this change. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Some tasks have Document Locations that reference the existing codebase. This is incorrect. The Document Locations sections should reference external locations, such as website documentation or Context7 and DeepWiki MCP tool documentation for libraries that are required for the task's implementation to ensure the correct calls are used. The subtask itself should state which files are required to be read or edited to complete the subtask. Please correct this and commit after making any changes. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best.

It looks like we are referencing libraries in the tasks without explicitly stating where the documentation is available. Please update the Documentation Locations for each task and ensure that every single external library has the correct documentation location, whether that is an MCP tool such as Context7 and DeepWiki or an explicit web address. The best option for cucumber is a subpath of https://cucumber.io/docs/guides/. You MUST check the documentation yourself before adding it so you can explain the reason for using it and provide detailed information. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Does all the functionality, including the happy path, error cases, and corner cases, have corresponding tests planned to be implemented? If not, please add them as new tasks or subtasks as required. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

In various tasks you have a single subtask for a number of tests to write or change, or vague test requirements. This is ambiguous. Please separate these out to separate subtasks and be explicit about what needs updating - For each task, seperate each required test into it's own subtask. Include test type, location, description & purpose. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Please ensure any task writing Jest tests includes references to Context7 tool Jest documentation, and any task that includes cucumber tests references cucumber at https://cucumber.io/docs/guides/ within the Documentation Locations section of each relevant task. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Does all the functionality, including the happy path, error cases, and corner cases, have corresponding tests planned to be implemented? If not, please add them as new tasks or subtasks as required. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Please ensure every task writing Jest tests includes references to Context7 tool Jest documentation, and every task that includes cucumber tests references cucumber at https://cucumber.io/docs/guides/ within the Documentation Locations section of each relevant task. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Prove that the story's planned taskproof steps are actually runnable at the point they would be reached. For every planned validation approach, confirm whether the necessary runtime, harness, dependencies, and scripts will exist by then. If not, update the story to add the missing prerequisite work and make the task proof path realistic. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

A Task's Subtask section should not contain subtasks to build code or run tests unless there is a specific need to, this is because all building and testing should be executed once all subtasks of a task are completed and the developer has moved on to the testing section, which must contain builds and running tests. Likewise the testing section should not contain steps to write code. Please move any build or test commands from within the subtask list to the Testing section, and remove any code writing steps from the testing section to the Subtask section of each Task. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Please ensure that each task, the testharness required for each testing step is available and functional at the point the test is to be executed. Tests that would be impossible to run should be removed, but if a testing step would be runnable, then it must stay. Do not provide options or ask questions, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Perform a repository ownership audit over the finalised task list before the final review passes. Every task, subtask, testing step, and final validation step MUST name exactly one repository, following the current `plan_format.md`. If the story spans multiple repositories, no single task may contain work for more than one repository; split any mixed task into separate repository-specific tasks and update the sequencing and cross-references accordingly. If a task in one repository depends on work in another repository, make that dependency explicit in the tasking. If the story affects only one repository, still ensure the repository is named consistently everywhere. Commit after making any changes.

Perform a pre-implementation review of the plan as if you are an external reviewer trying to find the issues that would normally only be discovered after implementation. Re-read the Description, Acceptance Criteria, Out Of Scope, Questions, every Task, every Subtask, every Testing step, and every Documentation Locations section. Identify any missing work, hidden dependencies, invalid assumptions, missing edge cases, missing error or recovery paths, missing env/config/docs/deployment changes, missing observability proof, or places where a reviewer would later say the tasking did not fully cover the story. Update the plan so each issue is resolved by either adding or updating tasks, subtasks, testing steps, or by making the scope decision explicit in the plan itself. Do not leave open review comments or TODO placeholders. Commit after making any changes.

Now perform a traceability and proof pass over the finalised plan. For every Acceptance Criterion, every important requirement stated in the Description, and every explicit Out Of Scope boundary, confirm that the plan contains: 1. the task or subtasks that implement it, 2. the testing step or steps that prove it, and 3. any required regression, error-case, and corner-case coverage. If any item is not fully implemented and provable from the task list, update the plan so the missing work and proof are added in the correct place. Ensure the final story validation proves the whole story rather than only isolated task-level behaviour. Commit after making any changes.

Please re-read the full task list and update the plan's `### Additional Repositories` section so it exactly matches every non-current repository that the planned tasks, subtasks, testing steps, and final validation steps will change. Add any missing repository, remove any repository that is no longer updated by the plan, keep the current repository out of that list because it is already implicit, and ensure the remaining repository names and paths stay consistent with the task ownership described throughout the plan. Commit after making any changes.
